subject: Geography
title: The Crystal Cave of Naica
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Crystal Cave of Naica.

hook.text: Mexico's Naica mine holds crystals 12 meters long—the largest natural crystals ever found.

buildup.text: Selenite crystals grew in a flooded chamber heated to 58°C by volcanic magma below.

discovery.text: Miners pumped the cave dry in 2000 and stumbled into a chamber of giant crystal beams.

twist.text: The temperature is so extreme that humans can only survive inside for 10 minutes without cooling suits.

climax.text: The cave was re-flooded to preserve the crystals. They may never be visited again.

punchline.text: Earth's most stunning cave is too hot and too precious to enter.

quiz.question: Why was the Crystal Cave of Naica re-flooded?
options: To preserve the crystals from deterioration
It was too dangerous to keep dry
